thoughts_srcservices_2067824716908.content: Creating services per request (e.g. `createNotificationService()` in route handlers) resets in-memory state and causes data loss across requests.

facts_srcservices_2067826515821.content: A singleton store module-level (`let store = ...`) is initialized once at load time; if that store is later replaced by reassignment inside a factory function, callers still hold the old reference and

conventions_srcservices_2067827846775.content: Prefer singleton class instances (shared `static` field) over module-level `let` variables for shared mutable state — the class field is stable and won't be shadowed.

thoughts_srcservicesnotificationservicet.content: Calling createNotificationService() at request time creates a fresh in-memory store per request — data written in one handler is invisible to another.

facts_srcservicesnotificationservicets_2.content: Use a singleton service instance (top-level `let service`) initialized once, not a factory called per request, when the store is in-memory.

conventions_planmd_step_ordering_2947537.content: TDD ordering: write tests first per area, then implementation, per step in the plan.

thoughts_srcroutesnotificationstestts_30.content: A review-fix run introduced new TS errors in test files that weren't in the original build failures. Fixing one thing can break another.

thoughts_notificationservicetestts_30414.content: A 'uses default store' test pollutes a singleton store, affecting subsequent tests. Tests sharing singleton state must clean up or isolate.

facts_test_mocking_3041443945032.content: When mocking NextRequest via `makeNextRequest` with spread, ensure all constructor arguments are accounted for or TS2554 errors occur.

thoughts_srcroutesnotificationstestts_33.content: Review-fix runs that touch test files can introduce new TypeScript compilation errors even while fixing runtime issues — always run build after fixing tests.

facts_srcservicesnotificationservicetest.content: Tests for singleton-based services (e.g. default store) that mutate global state must run last or be isolated with beforeEach/teardown to avoid polluting subsequent tests in the same file.

thoughts_srcservicesnotificationservicet.content: Patching a stub back to a real implementation at the end of a test without proper teardown causes singleton pollution that silently breaks unrelated tests in the same suite.

facts_srcservicesnotificationservicets_3.content: Request-time service instantiation (vs. module-level) was the correct fix for the original bug — it allows request-scoped overrides and prevents shared mutable state across concurrent requests.

conventions_srcroutesnotificationsts_331.content: Route handlers requiring auth should consistently apply `withAuth` middleware — PATCH and DELETE were missed while GET/POST had it.

thoughts_srcservices_3535594341829.content: ServiceResult union type members don't expose .val/.err properties; tests that access these must narrow the type first with guards or casts.

facts_testing_3535596405236.content: Co-located test files are typechecked as part of the project; test code must satisfy the same TypeScript constraints as production code.

facts_srcroutes_3535598110663.content: Handler call arity mismatches in route test files indicate the test is passing wrong number of arguments to the handler function.
